info.title: JSON Server API
info.description: Store and serve JSON documents over a tiny REST API. Public stores can be read without authentication; private stores and every write require a Bearer API key (format `json-server-xxxx`) that is linked to the store with the matching permission (`get` and/or `put`). CORS is enabled, so browser-based agents can call it cross-origin.

get.summary: Read a JSON store (optionally a nested value)
get.description: Returns the store's JSON content. Public stores need no auth; private stores require a Bearer key with `get` permission linked to the store. Use `path` to return only a nested value.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Dot/bracket selector to return just a nested value, e.g. `user[0].role.name` or `users.Thanh Khan`. Omit to get the whole document.

200.description: The stored JSON content, or the selected value when `path` is supplied.

put.description: Recursively merges the request body into the stored content: nested objects merge key by key, while arrays and primitive values are replaced. Requires a Bearer key with `put` permission linked to the store. Returns the merged document.

schema.description: Partial JSON to deep-merge into the existing content.

400.description: The request body was not valid JSON.

bearerAuth.description: API key in the format `json-server-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`. Create one in the dashboard, link it to the target store, and grant `get` and/or `put`. Send it as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

Unauthorized.description: Missing/invalid key, key lacks the required permission, key isn't linked to the store, or the key is locked.

NotFound.description: The store does not exist, or the `path` selector did not resolve.

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded — max 100 requests per minute per API key (private stores).

example.error: Rate limit exceeded. Max 100 requests per minute per API key.
